title: The New Pro Se: Generative AI and the Surge in Federal Civil Self-Representation
data:
O Cohen-Sasson- arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.29493, 2026
Since public access to generative AI tools became widespread, federal civil litigation 
has seen a marked increase in pro se (self-represented) plaintiffs. This paper 
analyzes that shift using~ 2.8 million filings, asking whether the post-GenAI period is 
associated not only with more pro se filings, but also with detectable changes in 
complaint text, litigation outcomes, and the composition of pro se litigants. Using civil 
filing data from FY2008-2025, we find that the federal civil pro se plaintiff rate rose

title: Business Informatics
data:
J KEPLER - 2026
This master's thesis investigates the potential and limitations of large language 
models (LLMs) for automated refactoring based on static code analysis. To this end, 
68 studies were systematically analyzed, central prompting approaches were 
structured, and empirically examined within a fully automated closedloop pipeline. 
The pipeline validates generated patches in multiple stages through compilation, test 
execution, and SonarQube re-scans. The evaluation is based on 146 real

data:
